macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Plunge on Tariff Policy and AI Sector Concerns
items.summary: Major US indices declined sharply with Dow Jones falling 1.66% to 48,804, S&P 500 down 1.04% to 6,837, and Nasdaq dropping 1.13% to 22,627 amid renewed worries over US tariffs and AI uncertainty. Treasury yields fell, with 10-year at 4.029% and 2-year at 3.483% as investors sought safety.

items.title: Gold Surges 2.5% as Safe Haven Amid Tariff Tensions
items.summary: Gold prices jumped nearly 2.5% to around $2,230, breaking resistance above $5,100, driven by US tariff news reactions from trading partners and potential return of volatile Chinese trading.

items.title: S&P 500 Futures Ease on Higher Rates and Tariff Headlines
items.summary: US stock futures softened with S&P 500 contracts lower as 10-year Treasury yield hovered near 4.05% and trade tensions including 15% tariff plans pressured sentiment. Dallas Fed Manufacturing Index showed stable factory conditions with firmer wages.

items.title: Bloom Energy Leads Gainers, IBM Top Loser in Volatile Session
items.summary: Bloom Energy surged 8.63% while AngloGold Ashanti rose 6.01% on strong Q4 earnings; PayPal gained 5.76% on takeover rumors. IBM plunged 13.15% on AI competition fears, with MongoDB and Datadog down over 11%.

items.title: Upcoming Fed Speakers and Economic Data in Focus
items.summary: Market awaits US PMIs, initial jobless claims, Richmond Manufacturing Index, and speeches from Fed members Waller, Cook, Barkin, and Collins, alongside SNB Chair comments.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: World Liberty Financial's USD1 Stablecoin Faces 'Coordinated Attack'
items.summary: World Liberty Financial, the Trump family-backed crypto venture, reported that its USD1 stablecoin briefly traded below $1 on Monday after what the project described as a coordinated attack involving hacked founder accounts and paid influencers spreading FUD. The stablecoin, with approximately $5 billion in market value, recovered to par after the incident.

items.title: Bitcoin Heads for Worst Month Since June 2022 Crypto Collapse
items.summary: Bitcoin extended its decline on Tuesday, putting the token on course for its steepest monthly drop since the industry's major downturn in June 2022.

items.title: Dragonfly Raises $650 Million Fourth Fund Amid VC Ecosystem Concerns
items.summary: Prominent venture firm Dragonfly, whose portfolio includes Polymarket and Ethena, has raised a $650 million fourth fund even as one of its partners predicted a 'mass extinction event' among the current VC ecosystem.

items.title: BlockFills Crypto Lender Halts Operations After $75 Million Loss
items.summary: Susquehanna-backed crypto lender BlockFills halted deposits and withdrawals after losing at least $75 million during the recent market downturn, echoing concerns from the 2022 crypto collapse.

items.title: Trump Family Hosts Mar-a-Lago Crypto Summit with Industry Leaders
items.summary: The Trump family convened a crypto summit at Mar-a-Lago with guests including Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong, Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon, and Binance founder CZ, described as a 'show of force' for World Liberty Financial, though its impact on stalled crypto legislation remains unclear.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: CrowdStrike Reports 89% Surge in AI-Powered Cyberattacks in 2025
items.summary: CrowdStrike's Global Threat Report 2026 reveals an 89% increase in attacks by AI-enabled adversaries, including use of machine learning and LLMs for phishing, malware development, and espionage campaigns by groups like Chinese intelligence and Russian Fancy Bear.

items.title: Chinese Institutions Advance AI for Predictive Surveillance and Policing
items.summary: Recent patents show Chinese entities developing AI systems using cameras, sound sensors, social media, and reports for real-time incident detection and predictive policing, with companies like Huawei contributing location-tracking neural networks.

items.title: Anthropic Exposes Large-Scale Model Distillation Attacks by Chinese AI Labs
items.summary: Anthropic identifies industrial-scale distillation attacks on Claude by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax, involving over 24,000 fraudulent accounts and 13 million exchanges to replicate model capabilities.

items.title: AI Arms Race Reduces Enterprise Breakout Time to 29 Minutes
items.summary: Adversaries are leveraging generative AI to accelerate attacks on enterprises, shrinking breakout times to just 29 minutes amid an intensifying AI arms race in cybersecurity.

items.title: Canada Considers Mandating AI Firms to Report Online Threats
items.summary: Following a mass killing linked to a ChatGPT user, Canada may require AI companies to notify police of threats, though experts note challenges in implementation as OpenAI faces scrutiny from officials.

items.title: Supermetrics Report: Only 6% of Marketers Fully Implemented AI
items.summary: New Supermetrics 2026 Marketing Data Report shows 80% of marketers face C-suite pressure to adopt AI, but only 6% have fully integrated it due to data access issues and lack of trust.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo Announces Surprise Treehouse Live Stream for February 24
items.summary: Nintendo revealed a Nintendo Treehouse Live Presentation on February 24 at 2PM PT / 10PM GMT, showcasing hands-on gameplay of Super Mario Bros. Wonder Nintendo Switch 2 Edition with Meetup in Bellabel Park expansion and Pokemon Pokopia, both upcoming Switch 2 exclusives.

items.title: Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition Now Available on Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: The Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition - Nintendo Switch 2 Edition launched digitally today, featuring enhanced action RPG gameplay, Skell piloting, and online squad features; existing Switch owners can upgrade via a pack.

items.title: Rise of Piracy Enters Early Access Today
items.summary: Rise of Piracy, a real-time action-adventure strategy game set in the high seas, launched in early access on PC on February 24, 2026.

items.title: TCG Card Shop Simulator Joins Xbox Game Pass Today
items.summary: TCG Card Shop Simulator became available on Xbox Game Pass for Series X|S, PC, and Cloud on February 24, offering management simulation gameplay.

items.title: New Xbox Sales Live with Discounts on Major Titles
items.summary: Xbox launched a new sale round-up on February 24, 2026, featuring big discounts on Atomic Heart, Dead Island 2, Saints Row IV: Re-Elected, and Metro Exodus.

items.title: Upcoming Releases Highlighted: Resident Evil Requiem Leads Week's Lineup
items.summary: This week's notable game releases include Nested Lands and City Hunter on February 25, Tales of Berseria Remastered on February 26, with Resident Evil Requiem as the most anticipated survival-horror blockbuster.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Trump Administration Pressures Tech Giants on Data Center Energy Costs
items.summary: The White House is asking Microsoft, Alphabet, and other major tech companies to sign public commitments pledging they will cover data center infrastructure costs to prevent electricity price increases for consumers. The pledges are non-binding but aim to address concerns about the AI infrastructure boom's impact on energy bills.

items.title: Tech Companies Offering Six-Figure Salaries for Communications Roles
items.summary: Major tech firms including Anthropic, Netflix, and OpenAI are hiring senior communications professionals at unprecedented salary levels, with positions ranging from $400,000 to $1.2 million. The trend reflects the industry's need for skilled communicators to explain complex technologies to regulators, employees, and customers.

items.title: China Targets Japan Defense Companies in Sweeping Export Ban
items.summary: China has announced an export ban targeting Japanese defense companies as part of broader trade tensions. The move reflects escalating geopolitical friction between the two nations.

items.title: Panama to Occupy Ports After Court Scraps CK Hutchinson Deal
items.summary: Panama is moving to occupy ports following a court decision that invalidated a deal with CK Hutchinson, marking a significant development in the country's port management strategy.
